content.claim: Early selective tracks in Hungary demonstrate higher value-added and student composition of higher status/skill, leading to increased inequality of opportunity, while students left in general tracks show weaker outcomes; this direct evidence comes from Hungary-specific panel data analysis.
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Cannot isolate selection effects from treatment effects (composition bias)
Weaker tests on general-track outcomes reduce confidence in losses claim
Panel data may not follow cohorts through complete educational career
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
